title: mahurea exstipulata subsp. duckei
intro: Mahurea exstipulata subsp. duckei, commonly known as Duckei's Mahurea, is a plant species of the Mahurea genus, native to the Amazon rainforest in South America. It is a shrub or small tree with medicinal properties, containing alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are known to possess antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Mahurea exstipulata subsp. duckei include the treatment of fever, inflammation, and infections, as well as its potential to act as a natural antiseptic and anti-parasitic agent. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used in folk medicine to promote healing and wellness in the region.
